dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9182 IH: Southeastern Rail Technologies Mapping Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Beyer (for himself, Mrs. Foushee, Ms. McClellan, Ms. Wilson of Florida, and Mr. Soto) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To require the Administrator of the Federal Railroad Administration to study improving rail and integrating new rail power technologies in the southeastern United States, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Southeastern Rail Technologies Mapping Act of 2026.

section.header: Study on improving rail and integration of new rail power technologies

subsection.text: The Administrator of the Federal Railroad Administration, acting through the Associate Administrator of the Office of Research, Data, and Innovation, shall conduct a study relating to the improvement of rail performance and integration of new rail power technologies in the southeastern United States.
In conducting the study under subsection (a), the Administrator shall—

subparagraph.text: any segment of a rail corridor between Florida and Washington, DC that may be suitable for technologies such as rail electrification or use of fuel cell or battery; and
any challenges relating to implementing such technologies; and

paragraph.text: for each segment of such rail corridor for which the Administrator determines the technologies described in paragraph (1) cannot be implemented—

subparagraph.text: explain the reasoning for such determination; and
recommend any update to the rail corridor that would make it suitable for the implementation of such technologies and provide an estimated cost for each such update.

subsection.text: Not later than 18 months after the date of enactment of this Act, the Administrator shall submit to Congress a report describing the results of the study conducted under subsection (a).
